{{Infobox group
| name = Sandvipers
| introduced = Soulsmith
}}

''See also: [[Sandvipers Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The Sandvipers are a sect of warriors characterized by their use of toxic, corrosive, and acidic powers. Their combat style involves [[madra]] that manifests as acid-green energy, capable of dissolving materials like wood or melting flesh. To manage the lethal nature of their own venomous abilities, members utilize a technique known as the [[Bloodforged Iron body]] and must consume the blood of actual sandvipers to regulate the toxins within their organs. This specialized path originated in the swamps as an adaptation to the corruption found in the Wilds. Members of the sect often possess serpent-like parasites or remnants attached to their bodies, such as those wrapped around their wrists, which can hiss or act as warnings. Physically, the group is often depicted wearing furs and animal hides, including those of bear-like creatures. The sect's hierarchy includes leaders like [[Gokren]] and [[Resh]], with their territory featuring a large, multi-peaked red tent for high-ranking members, while subordinates reside in simpler structures made of scavenged materials. While some view them as cowardly due to their reliance on poison, they are also recognized as competent hunters of beasts from the [[Desolate Wilds]].}}

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The Sandvipers are a tenacious sect of [[sacred beasts]] characterized by serpentine heads, centipede legs, and bright tan carapaces. As allies of [[Jai Sen]] with territory within the [[Five Factions Alliance]], they utilize scripted constructs for mining and employ prisoners who have lost duels or failed to pay debts to gather scales. These members, who wear blood-spattered furs and may have arrived via the [[Ruins]], possess insidious [[madra]] capable of dissolving living flesh like acid. Their combat capabilities include [[Ruler techniques]] that produce paralyzing gas, and they utilize the [[Bloodforged Iron body]] to resist their own toxins. During [[Lindon]]'s attempt to steal their scales, the group's guards were noted for using iron and scripted collars on captives while acting as escorts for mining teams.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] might take their embarrassment out on someone.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c5">''Soulsmith'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] were at the [[gate]] and are allies of [[Jai Sen]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c6">''Soulsmith'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] have a corner of the [[Five Factions Alliance]] territory.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c7">''Soulsmith'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] are members of the [[Sandviper sect]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] are a group that [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] encounter.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c8">''Soulsmith'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Jai Long]] melds into the crowd of [[Sandvipers]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c9">''Soulsmith'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] use captured prisoners to gather scales.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] use scripted constructs for mining.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] wear blood-spattered furs.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c10">''Soulsmith'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] are a group that takes people who cannot repay debts or lose duels.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c11">''Soulsmith'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|A Sandviper took [[Lindon]] in the middle of the night and brought him away from [[Fisher territory]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c12">''Soulsmith'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] possess insidious, venomous powers that can dissolve flesh like an acid.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandviper madra]] can burn through living flesh.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Sandviper [[Ruler techniques]] produce a gas that causes seizures and paralysis.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] are a group that uses specific techniques on prisoner flesh.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Sandviper guards use iron and scripted collars on prisoners.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] have a young chief.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Gesha considers the [[Sandvipers]] to be cowards.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Lindon]] has been tracking the shift changes of Sandviper guards working with mining teams.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c13">''Soulsmith'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] keep their scales in a secure location back in their main camp stronghold.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] are present in the surroundings.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] act as escorts for the [[miners]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c14">''Soulsmith'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Yerin]] fought off three Sandviper soldiers.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c15">''Soulsmith'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] are a group from which [[Lindon]] intended to steal scales.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] are tenacious.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] taste like chicken livers soaked in acid.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16">''Soulsmith'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|A [[sandviper]] is a natural sacred beast with centipede legs and a serpentine head.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[sandviper]] has a tan and bright carapace.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] may have found their way through the [[Ruins]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c18">''Soulsmith'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] use the [[Bloodforged Iron body]] to combat their own toxins.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Sandvipers]] are a group.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Sandvipers]] have miniature [[Remnants]] attached to their arms.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c19">''Soulsmith'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Blackflame ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The Sandvipers are fur-clad veterans of [[Gokren]]'s sect who travel using flying constructs or [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]] mounts. After their scouts spotted Arelius banners, the group gathered to watch the white spear and even intervened in battle using acid-green javelins. While their wagons were seen moving through the [[Desolate Wilds]], members of the group stayed behind to assist [[Jai Chen]] under the direction of [[Jai Long]]. Associated with both [[Jai Long]] and [[Gokren]], these warriors seek to capture [[Remnants]] for consumption while waging a guerilla war against the [[Jai clan]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Sandvipers]] are the veterans of [[Gokren]]'s sect.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c2">''Blackflame'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Scouts from the [[Sandvipers]] have spotted Arelius banners.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c3">''Blackflame'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sandvipers]] are fur-clad.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c4">''Blackflame'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|A Sandviper intervened in the battle by using acid-green javelins.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Sandvipers]] are a group that gathered to watch the white spear.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jai Long]] has four [[Sandvipers]] staying behind to take care of [[Jai Chen]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c6">''Blackflame'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The Sandviper wagon was seen in the [[Desolate Wilds]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Sandvipers]] travel on mounts such as Thousand-Mile Clouds or flying constructs.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c8">''Blackflame'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Sandvipers]] are associated with [[Jai Long]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c11">''Blackflame'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sandvipers]] are fur-clad and seek to capture [[Remnants]] for consumption.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Sandvipers]] are waging a guerilla war against the [[Jai clan]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c16">''Blackflame'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sandvipers]] accompanied [[Jai Long]] and [[Gokren]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c17">''Blackflame'', chapter 17</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Sandvipers]] were hiding nearby.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle groups]]
